account_structure.schwab_roth_ira: ~$4329.60 total — mostly long-term holdings + active W118 day trades (QTTB)
account_structure.schwab_margin: Active trading account — YMAT 87-share position held here
account_structure.webull_margin: $23 — small trades, subject to PDT (use sparingly)
account_structure.webull_cash: $31 — primary W118 day trading account (no PDT rule)

trades.notes: Clean W118 entry. Held well. First real win.
CHASED 30% above BUY signal. W118 BUY fired at $1.37, entered at $1.79. Classic bus-chasing mistake. Lesson: 3% rule — never enter more than 3% above signal.

trades.exit_reason: W118 SELL fired + K=100 overbought + halt resume
trades.notes: Entry was 12.9% above BUY signal ($8.37) — technically a chase but worked. Held through halt-up. Sold right when SELL fired. Textbook exit. Should have entered at $8.37 for +37% instead of +22%.
Quick scalp on QTTB re-entry. Clean in and out. 8 minutes.

Went to $11 after exit — missed $8.48 on 4 shares. Not a mistake to sell, just small position size. A+ entry was $5.54 premarket. Entered late at $9.42 (+69% from signal) but still caught +11%. Position sizing lesson: when setup is this clean from premarket, go bigger at the original signal.

trades.exit_reason: Manual cut after overnight hold — Pattern A fade, ZLSMA breakdown
trades.notes: Entered $1.49 without setting -8% stop ($1.371). Held overnight while stock broke below ZLSMA (Pattern A fade). Cut at $1.07 on May 27. Stop would have saved $26.19 — total cost $36.54 vs $10.35 with stop. THE airbag lesson. Never hold below ZLSMA. Never skip stop loss.

may_28_trades.exit_reason: Sold too early out of nerves — full exit at $2.90
may_28_trades.notes: Intuitive entry, clean setup from scanner. Sold all at $2.90 instead of trimming. Stock ran to $4.25 halt — T3 was $4.30. With T1/T2/T3 would have made $72 instead of $16. THE exit lesson. Next time: set T1 alert before buying, trim at each target.

may_28_trades.exit_reason: Quick exit, sold early before K hit 93
may_28_trades.notes: Rushed entry — grader said F (below ZLSMA at entry time). Entered on intuition, stock bounced back above ZLSMA and ran. Got lucky. Buy signal fired legitimately at ~$5.10 — entered before it at $5.33. Lesson: intuition finds the stock, wait for the actual signal.

may_28_trades.exit_reason: TradingView price alert at $2.75 triggered — sold all 9 shares green
may_28_trades.notes: Rushed entry before open. Held through pullback to $2.37 (near $2.355 stop, -8%). Patient wait paid off — TradingView alert at $2.75 fired, sold immediately and still green. Small win on a very stressful hold. Lesson: set your alert BEFORE you buy, let it do the emotional work for you.

paper_trades.notes: Paper trade. Stock ran +72% on day. Exited early at $0.44 — could have held to $0.47+. Good practice run on re-entry setup.

summary.best_trade: MASK Schwab Roth +$16.36 (stock halted at $4.25 halt, T3=$4.30 — missed $55 with T1/T2/T3 trimming)
summary.worst_trade: YMAT overnight hold -$36.54 (-28.2%) — missed stop loss, held below ZLSMA
summary.lesson_of_the_week: Three lessons burned in this week: (1) THE STOP LOSS IS THE AIRBAG — YMAT $36 loss vs $10 with stop. Never skip it, never hold below ZLSMA. (2) T1/T2/T3 EXITS — MASK ran to $5 after selling at $2.90. Trimming at targets = 4x more profit on same trade. Set targets BEFORE you buy. (3) WAIT FOR THE SIGNAL — NCT and QTEX both rushed before market open. Signal found the move; entering before it = gambling. Intuition finds the stock, the system fires the entry.
summary.note: Net negative due to YMAT -$36.54 overnight loss. All May 28 trades closed green. Goal win rate 80% — HIT exactly at 80% (8/10).
